How about an overweight to Healthcare in US equity allocations? This sector has drifted to a 58% discount to the S&P 500 on a price-to-sales ratio basis, even though the sector has the same profitability profile as the market (they both have a Return on Equity of 18.5%). With Healthcare's price-to-sales ratio spending many years drifting down to the current 1.47, suddenly the sector finds itself with a lower P/S ratio than it had in August 2008. In contrast, up up up went the S&P 500's Price-to-Sales ratio over all these years, to its current 3.50. It is reasonable to give Healthcare a look here.
